There’s also a paradox of a finite universe, which I’d be interested to see a video on. The paradox is the same as the logic of an infinite universe: if the conditions for the Big Bang can exist, then if you expand far enough, you might have the conditions for another big bang. Even with the concept of a heat death, the idea of there only being a single universe in.. ‘whatever existence is’, assumes that we’re truly special, which as we know isn’t the case in a universe with repeatable functions and conditions.

I hear people talk about the observable universe, but no one talks about the reachable universe. The longer we take to explore and spread ourselves out in the universe, the less of the universe is reachable. As space expands, things move further away. We have a sphere around us that we will never cross, and that sphere is shrinking every day. 73 5 replies

Hi Kurzgesagt, I’ve been a fan of your videos for a long time across different accounts and I’ve always loved your videos! I especially love the scientific and philosophical research in the content and all the artistic and musical dedication. In general, your work has been wonderful and I hope you continue to keep doing what you do! However, I have some feedback that I would like to state regarding your thumbnails, titles and some of the recent content. As has been documented in a few Reddit posts already, it’s been noticed that the original, very creative and introspective thumbnails and titles for certain videos have been scrapped and changed for more clickbait and less authentic versions (for example “What Are You Doing With Your Life? The Tail End” turning to “When This Number Reaches 5200– You Are Dead!” (Or something along those lines), and “You Are The Dream of a Dead Universe” to “We Did The Math— You Are Dead!”, as well as their thumbnails). While it is understood that the algorithm will recommend videos containing these less authentic methods of advertising, I feel like this is really a waste of talent from the artists as well as the old charm of an educational and deeper outlook of your videos. The channel is beginning to feel like a content farm similar to Bright Side, which is a bit saddening because of how much effort and love put into these videos made for smart content and the history with its audience. Sometimes they are directly misleading, for example “Why South Korea is Dying Out” has relatively little to do with the content of the video itself as it mentions it perhaps once or twice throughout the whole video. I just hope that we don’t stop the quality because of these facts and that the videos themselves don’t get affected as well. Maybe if it isn’t possible to keep the titles and thumbnails, would it be possible to at least make a video explaining the changes made recently by the channel? Just to give some more justification to the artists and authenticity of the messages you purvey and explain to the fans who have gotten disappointed due to them. However, I do understand the reasoning behind it all and I will support you no matter your publicity choices because I still love your content and they provide so much insight and information, especially to our younger generation! I just hope this message helps bring to light some of the problems some of us have noticed and helps you improve and keep up the awesome work!! Hegel's opinion on "infinity" in connection with cosmology. Hegel divided the concept of infinity into "bad infinity" and "real infinity". The cosmos consists of an infinite, cosmic vacuum and an infinite number of galaxies (stars, planets, quantum particles... etc). What is the difference? ... "Real infinity" is the infinite, eternal and absolute structure of the cosmic vacuum (everything exists in this structure). "Bad infinity" is the number of infinities that are bigger than others numbers (number on number, like a turtle on a turtle to infinity on which the Earth stands). In relation to cosmology "Bad infinity" is the number of galaxies (stars, planets, quantum particles... etc.). All galaxies are relative objects: they are born and end their existence (sooner or later)

What must be true for something to have a form? Must there not be a boundary — something that separates it from something else? But what could the universe be separated from? What exists outside of all that exists? And if something did exist outside — wouldn’t that too be part of the universe? If the universe includes everything that is, how can it have an outside? How can you assign a form to something you can never step outside of? Can a fish draw the shape of the sea it swims in? Can an eye see itself without a mirror? If the universe began as a singularity, it should have behaved like any other black hole. It would have eventually died, and the infinite curvature in spacetime would have "snapped back" rather violently initially (because spacetime is elastic and that initial drop from infinity to finiteness is technically an ∞% change compared to finite decreases afterwards) and then started gradually settling towards 0 curvature, like a springy rubber object being folded and then released. Except starting from infinite curvature, which looks more like all directions leading to the center rather than like a taco shape. What would essentially be happening is that an infinitely small circle would be expanding towards infinite area, and an infinite circle is a straight line. This would explain inflation, as that instant of infinite expansion from the elasticity of spacetime. It would explain the reason for and process of the big bang, as the idea of a dying black hole is at least known within our current physics. It would also explain the increasing expansion; there is not more mass-energy pushing the edges out faster. If a circle is expanding, the rate at which two points on the circumference appear to move apart will depend on how far apart they began; points that are further apart will experience a higher % change, and so things should appear to be accelerating away from us, and this perception should be true from any point of observation. It would give a possible solution to the Hubble tension, if different types of measurements have assumptions that affect how the method interacts with this geometry. This would imply that our black hole may have been embedded in a larger spacetime itself, and we are sort of resettling into that grid, possibly looking like a white hole from there. Is a white hole just a dying black hole? Interesting question for later. Maybe if a black hole we can observe dies we will watch a universe be born. On a very very different note, I've personally experienced some hallucinations and spiritual experiences (from TL seizures), and appeared to be in a place of "not existence or nonexistence" outside of the borders of reality. My initial sparking idea was that the basic unit of existence is reference, and therefore the most indivisible unit of information is self-reference (there can be "references", i.e. any type of relationship, between any objects or points in reality but they can be broken down into constituent parts of the separate objects, the rules governing them, etc). This would explain the strings of string theory; literally, the "object" at the bottom of everything is the concept of self-reference. The reasoning behind this is that if something is not referenced by any part of reality, including itself, by definition doesn't exist. Then the train of thought veered into Lovecraft tropes, that any type of description including "non-existent" is a form of reference, and so anything separate from our reality has to be outside our binary of existence and non-existence. And that is the "place" that I seemed to visit during my hallucinations. A Void on the outside of the reality loop, with many properties that frustratingly fit exactly the overused eldritch problem of being literally indescribable. It was quite comfortable being there, although I definitely can't recommend anyone having seizures.

When we look out into the universe, we are looking into the past. The further we look, the further back into the past we are looking all the way back to the singularity, all the way back to ground zero of the big bang explosion, the point from which it all emerged. If we had to define a center of the universe, then certainly this singularity, the ground zero of the big bang, is the best candidate for a center of the universe! It's as far back into the past as we could possibly look. The weird and paradoxical thing is that we are completely surrounded by this center of the universe. No matter which direction we look, we can look all the way back to the center of the universe. And if we had to define an edge of the universe, then certainly the present moment would be the best candidate for en edge. From our own perspective, it looks as though we are at the center of the universe, but in reality we are at the edge of the universe. And what is really the center of the universe, well it looks like the edge from our perspective.
